message:
test(core): PLANNER-3 wire regression coverage for INNER-join fast path

Integration tests for the PLANNER-3 gate-only wire landed in 527c2f87
(`prepare_simple_join_select_rows_with_scanner`). That commit consults
`fsqlite_planner::order_join_inputs_with_hints` using sqlite_stat1
row-count hints but intentionally defers the 5-parallel-structure
reshape (`table_sources`, `table_rows`, `join_plans`, `col_map` /
`col_collations`, `projection_indices`) to a follow-up. Without
regression coverage, a future reshape could silently produce wrong
results for inner joins or silently reorder a LEFT JOIN (which is
non-commutative and MUST stay in source order).

Three tests, all against the end-to-end SELECT path:

1. `inner_join_small_and_big_returns_same_rows_regardless_of_source_order`
   — creates `t_small` (10 rows) + `t_big` (10_000 rows) with referential
   integrity between them, runs ANALYZE, then issues the same inner join
   twice with `t_big JOIN t_small` and `t_small JOIN t_big` source
   orders. Both must produce the same canonicalized (sorted) result set.
   This guards the "small-joins-big" scenario the planner is expected
   to optimize once the reshape lands — and proves correctness at the
   gate-only stage.

2. `left_join_falls_through_planner_gate_without_reshape` — creates a
   LEFT JOIN with some matching and some non-matching rows (ref_id =
   999), runs ANALYZE, and checks that the result includes all left-
   side rows with NULL-padding for unmatched ones. This exercises the
   `all_inner_joins` flag: if the gate ever mis-classifies a LEFT JOIN
   as inner, the reshape (when landed) could drop unmatched rows.

3. `inner_join_without_analyze_preserves_source_order_result` — inner
   join on two tables with NO ANALYZE run. `sqlite_stat1_row_counts`
   returns an empty map, `has_stats` is false for both refs, and
   `order_join_inputs_with_hints` returns the identity permutation.
   Guards the empty-stats path against a regression that would
   mishandle it.

Test matrix
-----------
All 3 tests pass today (gate-only wire has identity permutations for
(1) and (3), and early-bails from the `all_inner_joins` check for (2)).
They will remain green under a correct reshape; a buggy reshape will
fail test (1) (wrong row count or wrong matches) or test (2) (row
count regression from LEFT JOIN mishandling).

Does not touch `connection.rs` — the gate-only wire is already
committed in 527c2f87. Pre-existing `--lib join` failures
(`test_zjisk1_strict_mode_rejects_join_fallback_dispatch` etc.) are
unrelated: they reproduce with zero working-tree changes on HEAD, so
they existed before PLANNER-3 wiring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
